commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is a concrete, narrow repair: preserve the Langfuse I/O fix while making root-run stamping use the correct run owner/span and adding regression coverage.; Needs attention: The diff handles opt-in captured prompt/response content and currently can attach that sensitive content to the wrong sibling run span.; Review finding: [P1] Key run I/O stamping by the run span, not the trace — extensions/diagnostics... (sha=afbffc06e2c2ec90fbd2b3672f5366923c9b75e8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds diagnostics-otel logic that mirrors model-call input/output onto the `openclaw.run` span for Langfuse trace and observation I/O display.

PR surface: Source +44. Total +44 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the reported single-run Langfuse symptom at source/proof level: current main only puts model I/O on model-call spans, and the PR body includes a live Langfuse API before/after check. I did not independently run Langfuse, so this is source-reproducible rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a concrete, narrow repair: preserve the Langfuse I/O fix while making root-run stamping use the correct run owner/span and adding regression coverage.

target.title: fix(sqlite): support Node 23.0–23.10 runtimes lacking StatementSync.columns()
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90007. After upgrading to 2026.6.1, the post-update `openclaw doctor` step crashed on **Node 23.0–23.10** with:

```
TypeError: statement.columns is not a function
```

across the plugin install index, task registry sidecar, and task flow sidecar migrations.

### Root cause

`StatementSync.prototype.columns()` (node:sqlite) was **Added in: v23.11.0, v22.16.0** (per the [Node docs](https://nodejs.org/api/sqlite.html#statementcolumns)). Because Node 22 is LTS and got the backport, the timeline is non-linear: `.columns()` exists on **22.16+** (so all of our `engines: >=22.19.0` floor), on **23.11+**, and on **24+** — but **not on 23.0 through 23.10**. The reporter's **Node 23.9.0** lands squarely in that hole, yet satisfies the declared engine, so it is a supported runtime.

Both SQLite execute paths called `statement.columns()` unconditionally to decide between `.all()` (result-returning) and `.run()`:

- `src/infra/kysely-sync.ts` — the sync helper used by every state migration
- `src/infra/kysely-node-sqlite.ts` — the Kysely driver `executeQuery`

### Fix

Centralize a single `executeCompiledQuerySync` in `kysely-node-sqlite.ts` (the sync helper and the async driver both delegate to it), feature-detect `.columns()`, and when it is absent classify result-returning statements from the **compiled query shape**:

- `SelectQueryNode` → rows
- `Insert/Update/DeleteQueryNode` → rows iff a `RETURNING` clause is present
- raw/DDL/transaction-control → leading-keyword + `RETURNING` heuristic

On Node 22.16+/23.11+/24+ the `.columns()` fast path is unchanged (the fallback is never evaluated). This also collapses the two previously-duplicated execute bodies into one path.

## Verification

Reproduced and verified against a standalone **Node v23.9.0** (the reporter's runtime) and local **Node v26**:

| | Node 23.9.0 (`columns` absent) | Node 26 (`columns` present) |
|---|---|---|
| **Before fix** | `TypeError: statement.columns is not a function` | ✅ works |
| **After fix** | ✅ all queries pass | ✅ all queries pass |

Exercised through both the sync helper (`executeSqliteQuerySync`, the path the migrations use) and the async Kysely driver: insert + `insertId`, select, `INSERT … RETURNING`, update, delete, `takeFirst`, raw pragma read/write, transaction rollback, savepoints, and streaming — identical results on both runtimes.

New regression tests simulate a `node:sqlite` build without `StatementSync.columns()` (Proxy that hides the method), so CI on Node 24 still exercises the fallback classifier through both the sync and driver entry points.

Local commands (normal source checkout):

```
pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/kysely-node-sqlite.ts src/infra/kysely-sync.ts src/infra/kysely-node-sqlite.test.ts
node scripts/check-kysely-guardrails.mjs
pnpm test src/infra/kysely-node-sqlite.test.ts src/infra/kysely-sync.types.test.ts \
          src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-store.test.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts
```

All green (kysely infra 7, types 1, doctor-state-migrations 51, installed-plugin-index 14).

Thanks to @TreyLawrence for the precise report and reproduction.
target.author: sjf
